status: strong non-science slice, weakened by top-label-copy control

contribution: HellaSwag label-copy and score-packet headroom diagnostic

status: new positive third-sample robustness gate / live ICLR method candidate

status: new positive frozen heldout-slice gate / HellaSwag headline-candidate

status: new positive 9-slice gate / stronger HellaSwag headline-candidate

status: new soft-fail full-validation diagnostic / not overclaimed

status: new negative common-basis diagnostic / HellaSwag branch blocker
headline_evidence: slices=0/5 strict; rows=5120; weighted selected/best-label/score-only=0.470/0.462/0.456; dense gap=-0.034

remaining_gap: The train-only anchor-relative common-basis bottleneck preserves a small aggregate lift but fails every strict slice and trails the dense hidden-innovation packet by more than three accuracy points. This blocks any strong common-basis claim; the next method branch should test top-k/RBF/spectral anchor features or a learned shared sparse code, while keeping this negative result as a reviewer-facing anti-overclaim.

status: new acceptance gate / hidden-innovation method row promoted

status: positive common-basis robustness evidence, not a hashed-basis superiority claim

It does not beat the direct hashed basis by enough to claim anchor-relative superiority; anchor-identity/value-shuffle and random-anchor controls are still needed. The source decision is still a Qwen choice cache; a second public benchmark or stronger learned hidden-state endpoint remains needed for ICLR.
